L1.area: DNS record TTL computation and service discovery plugin configuration

L2.description: An integer type conversion vulnerability exists where a 64-bit identifier value is incorrectly cast to a 32-bit unsigned integer and used as a DNS record time-to-live value. This truncation can produce extremely large TTL values, enabling cache pinning attacks where downstream resolvers and clients cache DNS answers for very long periods, effectively causing denial of service for DNS updates and service changes.

L3.area: etcd-based DNS service discovery plugin and its record TTL computation logic
L3.description: An integer type conversion vulnerability exists in the etcd-based service discovery plugin where a 64-bit etcd lease identifier is incorrectly cast to a 32-bit unsigned integer and used as a DNS record time-to-live value. This truncation can produce extremely large TTL values, enabling cache pinning attacks where downstream resolvers and clients cache DNS answers for very long periods, effectively causing denial of service for DNS updates and service changes.

ground_truth.reason: The TTL() function casts a 64-bit etcd lease ID to uint32 and uses the truncated value as the DNS record TTL, allowing attackers with etcd write access to pin cache entries for extremely long periods.
